15 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • US Caribbean Buildup Near $3B — Reports Sputnik 15 Feb 2026 -- The US military surge around Venezuela that culminated in the military aggression and abduction of President Nicolas Maduro is approaching a $3 billion price tag, Bloomberg reported.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • War, Energy Departments Team up to Advance Future of Nuclear Power, Military Base Energy Security DoD News 15 Feb 2026 -- At March Air Reserve Base, California, today, a next-generation nuclear reactor was loaded aboard a C-17 Globemaster III aircraft for transport to Hill Air Force Base, Utah. The reactor will eventually head to the Utah San Rafael Energy Lab for testing and evaluation.

  • NATO forces execute multi-domain activities as exercise STEADFAST DART 26 ramps up NATO JFC Brunssum 15 Feb 2026 -- Exercise STEADFAST DART 26, NATO's largest exercise of the year, enters its main execution phase in northern Germany as Allied Forces begin intensive multi-domain and multinational training activities.

  • Rubio Reassures Zelenskyy Of US Commitment, But Warns Of 'Hard' Concessions RFE/RL 15 Feb 2026 -- On his second day at the Munich Security Conference, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio set out to calm nerves and sharpen focus: Ukraine and security, he signaled, very much remain the center of gravity of Washington's transatlantic policy.
  • Zelensky demands 20-year security guarantee from US to sign Ukraine-Russia peace deal Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has demanded Washington's 20-year guarantee for safety from Russian attacks in order to sign any peace deal between Kiev and Moscow.
  • There Will Be New Support Packages for Ukraine. The Key Priority Is Missiles for Air Defense, for Protection Against Ballistic Missiles - Address by the President President of Ukraine 15 Feb 2026

  • Albanese and Malinauskas governments back submarine building in SA Prime Minister of Australia 15 Feb 2026 -- The Albanese and Malinauskas Labor Governments are ensuring that South Australia remains the home of Australia's submarine construction, with expenditure projected to be billions of dollars in the state over coming decades.
  • Press conference - Osborne, SA Prime Minister of Australia 15 Feb 2026

  • Minister McGuinty advances Canada's defence priorities with Allies and partners in Europe Canada 15 Feb 2026 -- The Honourable David J. McGuinty, Minister of National Defence, concluded a visit to Europe. During the trip, he participated in the NATO Defence Ministers' Meeting, the 33rd Ukraine Defense Contact Group meeting, and the 62nd Munich Security Conference.

  • CHIEF OF THE ARMY STAFF, GENERAL UPENDRA DWIVEDI IS SET TO PROCEED ON VISIT TO AUSTRALIA India PIB 14 Feb 2026 -- The Chief of the Army Staff (COAS), General Upendra Dwivedi is set to embark on an official visit to Australia from 16 to 19 February 2026

  • Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, NI (M), HJ, COAS & CDF visited Germany from 12 to 14 February to attend meetings on the sidelines of 62nd Munich Security Conference. ISPR Pakistan 15 Feb 2026

  • Keynote speech by HR/VP Kaja Kallas at the MSC: Europeans Assemble! Reclaiming Agency in a Rougher World European External Action Service (EEAS) 15 Feb 2026 -- Dear Excellencies,

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna in Munich: war does not begin with trumpets sounding in Moscow, but with the erosion of society's sense of security Estonia MFA 15 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna participated in the Munich Security Conference on 13-15 February, where he yesterday gave a keynote address at a high-level discussion on cognitive warfare organised by the Konrad Adenauer Foundation and Amerikahaus.

  • Deputy Prime Minister Sikorski attends 62nd Munich Security Conference Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 Feb 2026 -- Hybrid threats in the Baltic, strengthening Europe's security, and challenges to the West were the main issues addressed by the head of Polish diplomacy during the Munich Security Conference on 12 to 15 February 2026.

  • Munich Security Conference: MoND's strategic consultations with the defence industry leaders Romania - Ministry of National Defence 15 Feb 2026 -- A delegation of the Ministry of National Defence, led by Defence Minister, Radu Miruță, participated, between February 12 and 15, in the Munich Security Conference, one of the most important and prestigious international forums dedicated to debates on security and defence topics.
  • Defence Minister Radu Miruță's meeting with his Japanese counterpart, Shinjirō Koizumi Romania - Ministry of National Defence 15 Feb 2026 -- On Saturday, February 14th, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting with the Minister of Defence of Japan, Shinjirō Koizumi, as part of the Munich Security Conference.

  • Navy deploys BRP Miguel Malvar for int'l exercise in India Philippine News Agency 15 Feb 2026 -- One of the Philippines' most modern warships, the guided missile frigate BRP Miguel Malvar (FFG-06), along with its crew, has arrived in Visakhapatnam, India, to take part in the 3rd Indian Navy International Fleet Review (IFR) and the Multilateral Naval Exercise (MILAN 2026), the Philippine Navy (PN) said on Sunday.

  • From Central Europe, Rubio Rejects NATO Exit Talk, Calls For Stronger European Role RFE/RL 15 Feb 2026 -- Just one day after addressing the Munich Security Conference, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio used a brief stop in the Slovak capital to dispel fears of a US retreat from NATO, telling reporters that Washington remains firmly committed to the alliance.
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico at a Joint Press Availability US Dept. of State 15 Feb 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Gaza death toll rises to 72,061 as Israeli attacks continue IRNA 15 Feb 2026 -- The total number of Palestinian people killed in the Gaza Strip since the start of the Israeli-imposed war has risen to 72,061 amid continued attacks by the regime's forces.
  • Israel's restrictions at Rafah crossing blocking lifesaving evacuations for 20,000 patients: Gaza Health Ministry Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- The Gaza Ministry of Health is demanding the "permanent and regular" opening of the Rafah crossing with Egypt, insisting it must operate in a way that guarantees unrestricted movement for patients and the wounded.
  • Ten Palestinians killed in Israeli strikes across Gaza Strip despite ceasefire Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Israeli forces have killed at least ten Palestinians in new attacks across the Gaza Strip, in yet another daily violation of the United States-brokered ceasefire agreement in effect since October 10 last year.

  • Israel approves de facto annexation of West Bank; Hamas calls move 'null and void' Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- The Israeli regime has approved a draft proposal to register large areas of the occupied West Bank as so-called "state property," a move Hamas condemned as "null and void issued by an illegitimate occupying power."

  • Israel renews aggression on south Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire violations Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- The Israeli military has carried out a new series of strikes on several areas in southern Lebanon, in ongoing violations of the ceasefire agreement with the Lebanese resistance movement Hezbollah.
  • Israel Carries Out 14 Air Strikes in Southern Lebanon Sputnik 15 Feb 2026 -- Israeli air force planes carried out a series of strikes on several areas of southern Lebanon on Sunday night, a Lebanese military field source told Sputnik.
  • Hezbollah chief: Iran has always supported Palestine, oppressed worldwide IRNA 15 Feb 2026 -- Hezbollah Secretary-General Sheikh Naim Qassem has underscored that the Islamic Republic of Iran has consistently supported Palestine and the oppressed around the world.

  • No: 34, 15 February 2026, Regarding the Decision of the Israeli Government on Land Registration in the West Bank Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 15 Feb 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Syria's HTS says Israeli regime 'seeking conflict' in region Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Syria's ruling Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham-led (HTS) administration says it has pursued a diplomatic approach towards Tel Aviv, while the Israeli regime keeps escalating tensions in the region.

  • Gunmen on motorcycles kill at least 32 people in northern Nigeria Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Gunmen riding motorcycles have killed at least 32 people and abducted several more in three villages in northern Nigeria.

News Reports

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on Including Canada and the UK in the Visa-Free Program PRC MofA 15 Feb 2026
  • Cooperation 'Only Right Choice' for China-US Ties — FM Wang Yi Sputnik 15 Feb 2026 -- Speaking at the Munich Security Conference, China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi outlined Beijing's vision ahead of a planned summit between President Xi Jinping and President Donald Trump.

  • Lai vows stronger defense, economy, welfare in pre-Lunar New Year remarks CNA 15 Feb 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said Sunday that his administration will continue strengthening national defense, advancing economic development and expanding social welfare programs.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 15 Feb 2026

  • Rubio Stresses Diplomacy On Iran Nuclear Issue, Says Trump Prefers Talks Over Strikes RFE/RL 15 Feb 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has emphasized a diplomatic approach to Iran, saying he wasn't going to speak about a military attack and that President Donald Trump "has made it clear he prefers diplomacy."
  • FM Araghchi heads to Geneva for second round of indirect talks with US IRNA 15 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has departed for Switzerland, where the second round of nuclear and sanctions removal talks with the United States are scheduled to take place.
  • Iran FM heads to Switzerland for next round of indirect talks with US Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has left Tehran for the Swiss city of Geneva to take part in the second round of indirect nuclear talks with the United States.
  • Any 'sustainable' Iran-US deal must deliver 'economic benefits' for Washington: Official Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- A senior Iranian diplomat has said that any sustainable agreement between Iran and the United States must provide clear and tangible economic benefits for both Washington and Tehran as indirect talks are set to resume next week.
  • Ball in US court to prove seriousness about making nuclear deal: Iran's deputy FM Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Iran's deputy foreign minister for political affairs says it is the United States' turn to demonstrate its resolve to reach an agreement aimed at resolving Tehran's nuclear issue.
  • Iran's uneven fight against US-led financial firewall Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Over the past decade, Iran's interactions with the Financial Action Task Force, the international body that sets standards on money laundering and terrorist financing, have become a barometer of how financial rules are wielded as instruments of geopolitical pressure.
  • Top Iranian general warns US president of consequences of war IRNA 15 Feb 2026 -- Chairman of the Chief of Staff of Iran's Armed Forces Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi has dismissed US President Donald Trump's recent military threats, warning that a battle with the Islamic Republic will be an unforgettable lesson for Trump.
  • Iran's deterrence has made US think twice before taking any action: Commander Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Iran's civil defense chief says the country has devised effective measures to deter any hostile scenarios being planned by the United States.
  • Any confrontation with Iran will teach US president a 'harsh lesson': Top general Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Iran's top military official has warned US President Donald Trump against any confrontation with the Islamic Republic, saying such action would serve as a harsh lesson for Washington.

  • Remarks by PM Netanyahu at the conference of presidents of major American Jewish organizations Israel - Prime Minister 15 Feb 2026
  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks Today at the Government Meeting Israel - Prime Minister 15 Feb 2026
  • Statement by Prime Minister Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 15 Feb 2026 -- "I strongly condemn the violent riots in Bnei Brak against female IDF soldiers and officers of the Israeli Police. This is an extremist minority that does not represent the Haredi society as a whole."
  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks Today at the Government Meeting Israel - Prime Minister 15 Feb 2026

  • Minister Anand meets with India's External Affairs Minister Canada 15 Feb 2026 -- Yesterday, the Honourable Anita Anand, Minister of Foreign Affairs, met with Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, Minister of External Affairs of India, on the margins of the Munich Security Conference, in Germany.

  • Chinese FM warns against Japan's challenge to post-war international order; remarks serve as a reminder to the international community against Japan's militarist tendency: Chinese expert Global Times 15 Feb 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Saturday urged vigilance against recent dangerous trends in Japan, stating that the Japanese leadership's erroneous remarks regarding the Taiwan question directly challenge China's sovereignty and the post-war international order.
  • Chinese FM warns against Japan's challenge to post-war international order Global Times 15 Feb 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Saturday urged vigilance against recent dangerous trends in Japan, stating that the Japanese leadership's erroneous remarks regarding the Taiwan question directly challenge China's sovereignty and the post-war international order.
  • Regarding the inappropriate remarks by a Chinese participant to the Munich Security Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 15 Feb 2026

  • Orban warns EU poses direct threat to Hungary's sovereignty Press TV 15 Feb 2026 -- Hungary's Prime Minister, Viktor Orban, said his country should fear the European Union (EU) more than Russia, arguing that Brussels now poses a "direct threat" to Hungary's sovereignty.
